terminationNotice.description: Notice period for convenience termination should be 30 days. Shorter or immediate termination is unfavourable; longer notice (90+ days) is unusual.

paymentPeriod.description: Standard invoice payment term is Net 30 days. Net 60 or Net 90 are unfavourable for service providers, while Net 15 or immediate is favourable.

liabilityCap.description: Limitation of liability capped at 1x the fees paid under the contract is market standard. Unlimited liability or extreme caps are unfavourable.

governingLaw.description: Governing law should align with the main operational territory. Foreign jurisdictions (e.g. outside the US/UK/Local) are unusual or unfavourable.

ipOwnership.standard: Customer owns work product; Vendor retains pre-existing IP
ipOwnership.description: Customer should own deliverables they paid for. Vendor must keep their background tools and libraries. Vendor owning all work product is unfavourable.

indemnity.standard: Mutual IP infringement and third-party claims only
indemnity.description: Indemnity should be mutual and capped, limited to intellectual property claims. Uncapped or one-sided general indemnity is unfavourable.

confidentiality.description: Confidentiality must bind both parties (mutual). Term should be limited rather than indefinite, except for trade secrets.
